title: eriogonum niveum subsp. decumbens
intro: Eriogonum niveum subsp. decumbens, also known as the snow buckwheat or white sagebrush, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the buckwheat family. This shrub-like plant is native to the mountainous regions of the southwestern United States and has been used for centuries in traditional medicine. The medicinal properties of Eriogonum niveum subsp. decumbens include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which have been used to treat a variety of health issues, such as wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of Eriogonum niveum subsp. decumbens can be made into teas, infusions, and topical ointments to harness its health benefits, including reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting immune function.
